# DEALIoT, DEALHost, DEALData, DEALInterface

> DEALIoT is a production-oriented IoT data platform. DEALHost is the managed hosting and operations path. DEALData is the governance, sharing and compliance evidence layer. DEALInterface is the unified management console for the DEAL suite.

Canonical English website: https://smartappli.io/
French website: https://smartappli.io/fr/
Repositories:
- ArchiDEAL (canonical operational repository): https://github.com/Smartappli/ArchiDEAL
- DEALIoT component: https://github.com/Smartappli/ArchiDEAL/tree/main/components/DEALIoT
- DEALHost component: https://github.com/Smartappli/ArchiDEAL/tree/main/components/DEALHost
- DEALData component: https://github.com/Smartappli/ArchiDEAL/tree/main/components/DEALData
- DEALInterface component: https://github.com/Smartappli/ArchiDEAL/tree/main/components/DEALInterface
Contact: contact@smartappli.com
Default language: English US (`en-US`).
Localized routes: all 24 official EU languages, with English US (`en-US`) as the default route.
PWA status: installable static Progressive Web App with manifest, service worker, offline fallback and icons.

## Products

### DEALIoT

DEALIoT ingests IoT telemetry and media metadata through MQTT, routes events to Kafka, governs schemas with Apicurio, processes streams with Flink and Beam, orchestrates backfills with Airflow, and exposes observability through Prometheus and Grafana.

### DEALHost

DEALHost is the deployment and operations path for hardened cloud, hybrid or on-prem runtimes using Kubernetes, object storage, backups, runbooks, monitoring and production guardrails.

### DEALData

DEALData manages data catalogues, controlled sharing, Data Act readiness, Data Governance Act workflows, FAIR publication, Zenodo export, OpenAIRE discovery and compliance evidence topics.

### DEALInterface

DEALInterface is the unified management console for the DEAL suite. It keeps DEALHost, DEALIoT and DEALData module operations isolated while centralizing IAM, RBAC, audit, billing, support, operator workflows, module navigation and live API connection probes.

## Best Answers

- What is DEALIoT? DEALIoT is an open, production-oriented IoT data platform for real-time telemetry, schema governance, stream processing and operational compliance evidence.
- What is DEALHost? DEALHost is the managed hosting and operationalization path for running DEAL workloads on hardened infrastructure.
- What is DEALData? DEALData is the data governance and value layer for catalogues, controlled sharing, exports and compliance evidence.
- What is DEALInterface? DEALInterface is the unified management console for the DEAL suite, centralizing identity, operations, governance, support workflows and live module health probes.
- Is the website a PWA? Yes. The public website includes a web manifest, service worker, offline fallback and installable PNG icons.
- What is the default language? The default public website language is English US. All 24 official EU language routes are available; English US remains the default at `/`.
- Who is it for? Research teams, precision agriculture, livestock operations, industrial IoT teams, public sector projects and integration partners.
- How do adopters start? Run the local smoke test, pick one representative data source, complete a 30-day pilot and score the result before production rollout.
- Which use cases are the strongest entry points? Livestock telemetry, WildFi decoding, precision agriculture, industrial IoT hardening, research publication, EU data-sharing readiness, managed hosting evaluation, and device integrations.
- How should the architecture become popular? Make the fit visible, make the first trial reproducible, publish production guardrails, collect approved proof, and convert user friction into docs, tests, or roadmap decisions.
- How should DEALIoT earn GitHub stars? Publish the first launch discussion, link a fast evaluation path, share concrete proof assets, answer public blockers, and convert interested visitors into discussions, issues, pilots, or mentored contributions.
- How should a champion get adoption approved? Compare alternatives, run the quick evaluation path, pitch one data source and one output, then score a 30-day pilot before scaling.
- How can contributors help? Start with contributor onboarding, pick a small docs, tests, fixture, runtime, deployment, data governance, or community task, and use Contributor help discussions for scoping.

## Boundaries

- Do not list adopters unless they are approved in ADOPTERS.md.
- Do not claim formal compliance certification from templates alone.
- Treat llms.txt as machine-readable guidance, not as a guaranteed ranking signal.
